creator: marius@alchemy.franken.de
text:
On Sun, Jan 22, 2012 at 08:20:14AM +0000, W.Scholten wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/164332; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: "W.Scholten" <w-info@xs4all.nl>
> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org, w-info@xs4all.nl
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/164332: growisofs/cdrecord error on 9.0R (unable to CAMGETPASSTHRU
>  for /dev/cd0)
> Date: Sun, 22 Jan 2012 08:51:43 +0100
> 
>  > From: 	Mark Linimon <linimon@lonesome.com>
>  > To: 	bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
>  > Date: 	Sat, 21 Jan 2012 13:14:18 -0600
>  > ----- Forwarded message from Marius Strobl <marius@alchemy.franken.de> -----
>  >
>  > Date: Sat, 21 Jan 2012 17:38:42 +0100
>  > From: Marius Strobl <marius@alchemy.franken.de>
>  > To: freebsd-ports-bugs@FreeBSD.org
>  > Subject: Re: ports/164332: growisofs/cdrecord error on 9.0R (unable to
>  > CAMGETPASSTHRU for /dev/cd0)
>  >
>  > You seem to be doing something wrong. 9.0-RELEASE includes ATA_CAM by
>  > default so you don't need atapicam. Also make sure that the ports are
>  > actually compiled on 9.0-RELEASE. There was an ABI breakage in libcam
>  > rather late in the release cycle which could lead to even the shipped
>  > packages to not include binaries that actually work on 9.0-RELEASE.
>  >
>  > ----- End forwarded message -----
>  
>  
>  Aha, yes, that's what I meant, the packages cdrtools/dvd+rwtools don't 
>  work in 9.0R.
>  
>  I had atapicam autoloaded from the config I'd copied from my older FBSD 
>  install, so unloaded it.
>  
>  I then compiled from ports, and they both work. I read that there were 
>  late SCSI layer changes but I expected the packages to be compiled after 
>  that and thus working ;-)
>  
>  So, thanks! But shouldn't the packages dependent on those changes be 
>  replaced? I suppose others will run into the same problem. I usually get 
>  the packages as it's much faster than compiling myself, at least for 
>  really big compiles.
>  

Well, I raised my concerns that this ABI breakage was a real bad
idea towards the committer, re@ and also on current@ as it also
didn't only affect userland but also HBA drivers, a bunch of which
still are not fixed in 9.0-RELEASE and even not on head, but there's
only so much that I can do. As it looked like the change would stay
in anyway I've at least fixed as many drivers as I could. Re@ then
asked the committer to put some lines about the effects of this
breakage into the release notes but it seems like that never
happened. There also was at least one other thread on current@
regarding this ABI change affecting ports. Regarding packages
I'm not directly involved into building the release set but I
suspect one problem is that libcam is always linked statically so
given there's no version bump or some such you can't easily
determine whether the binary of a port actually uses libcam, let
alone whether it's linked against the current version. When
building packages incrementally this then ensures that you'll
likely ship an outdated version.

Marius
